title: Make Simple Assertions
description: Learn to use Playwright's assertion library for verifying test expectations
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Navigates to a webpage
2. Asserts an element is visible
3. Asserts an element contains specific text
4. Asserts the URL matches expected value
5. Asserts an element is not visible

starterCode.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_simple_assertions(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://demo.playwright.dev/todomvc')
    
    # TODO: Assert the input field is visible
    
    # TODO: Add a todo item 'Learn Playwright'
    
    # TODO: Assert the todo text is visible
    
    # TODO: Assert URL contains 'todomvc'
    
    # TODO: Assert completed todos section is not visible initially
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect
import re

def test_simple_assertions(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://demo.playwright.dev/todomvc')
    
    input_field = page.get_by_placeholder('What needs to be done?')
    expect(input_field).to_be_visible()
    
    input_field.fill('Learn Playwright')
    input_field.press('Enter')
    
    expect(page.get_by_text('Learn Playwright')).to_be_visible()
    expect(page).to_have_url(re.compile('.*todomvc'))
    
    expect(page.locator('.clear-completed')).not_to_be_visible()

hints: Use .not before an assertion to negate it
Regular expressions work with toHaveURL and toHaveText
